title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A collision on Interstate 81 in Virginia killed five Massachusetts residents, including a Greenfield family and a Worcester woman.
Virginia State Police charged bus driver Jing S. Dong with involuntary manslaughter following the crash involving multiple vehicles.
The crash prompted a GoFundMe to support the funeral expenses of the Doncev family, members of the Providence Christian Academy community.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong had a pivotal 3-RBI night in the Red Sox's recent victory over Cleveland.
Wong's performance comes amid a diminished role, as Mickey Gasper is named the primary catcher.
Despite a .245 average, Wong's contributions position him as a potential secondary catcher for the team.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings with seven strikeouts, earning his sixth win in the Red Sox's 9-1 victory over the Guardians.
Gray has maintained a strong performance in May with a 2.00 ERA across five starts, contributing to Boston's rotation amidst injuries.
The veteran pitcher holds a 3.06 ERA for the season, with Boston achieving a 7-3 record in his starts.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game at Polar Park in Worcester features Patriots' coaches as team captains.
Celebrity participants include Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, and comedians Matt Rife, Dave Portnoy.
The event supports the MayeDay foundation, aiding children and families facing hardship.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots reportedly have trade framework in place to acquire Eagles' receiver A.J. Brown.
A post-June 1 trade would reduce Eagles' salary cap hit from $43.5 million to $16 million.
Disagreement remains over Patriots' unwillingness to exchange a first-round pick in the reported deal.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Residents in Greater Boston reported hearing a loud boom on Saturday afternoon, causing speculation on social media.
Meteorologists suggest a bolide meteor likely caused the sonic boom heard east of Boston, ruling out an earthquake.
Public safety officials confirmed no emergency incidents connected to the boom, despite numerous 911 calls in the region.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, resulting in a 4-3 loss for the Red Sox against the Guardians.
Brayan Bello pitched seven scoreless innings in relief, striking out five and allowing no walks or runs.
The Red Sox are 23-33, last in the AL East, after losing six of their last seven games.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and TD Garden's passionate environment on a recent livestream.
The Celtics and Miami Heat are leading contenders for Antetokounmpo, though Miami is favored.
Speculation involves a potential trade of Jaylen Brown to acquire Antetokounmpo, possibly in a three-team deal.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police ended the Harvard Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd and swimming attempts in the Charles River.
Massachusetts State Police, along with Cambridge and Harvard police, intervened around 5 a.m. to clear the gathering.
The event followed Harvard's commencement for the Class of 2026, where Conan O'Brien delivered the keynote address.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair', featuring Uma Thurman, is now streaming on Peacock.
'Spider-Noir', starring Nicolas Cage as a 1930s Spider-Man, is available on Prime Video.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, with Tina Fey and Will Forte, is streaming on Netflix.
